item.title: Gardening, 15th June
item.description:
This garden not cut /mow or tidyed up when gardeners go on this lawmmower drove on but full of rubbish and after all over pavement .

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole getting bigger, 15th June
item.description:
These pot holes are getting worse , I reported this last week ,got a email back that the issue has been resolved Reffence no 9595704 it clearly hasn’t , as you can see by the photos , they filled 2 tiny holes to the right , but not the main issue opposite my house 53 , as i reported last time I have nearly twisted my ankle getting out the car and crossing over when it’s dark , its absolutely disgraceful , to tell me it’s been resolved

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Granville Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes badly repaired, 15th June
item.description:
For the five years I have lived on Highview Road I have been in despair at the appalling condition of the road surface. When potholes have been repaired, invariably the repair has been ruined by leaving one poorly finished edge (usually because one pothole is next to another). Within weeks the repairs degrade to the point that stones and hardcore start to be scattered across the road. The potholes get worse and the stones are never cleared up. Now our road is closed for gas works for several weeks. Over a dozen huge holes many feet wide four to six feet deep have been dug for the gas works. As these holes are filled I am dismayed to see them immaculately finished. Dismayed because that means there is no intention to resurface the entire road. As I observe the work being finished, I am waiting for someone to take the initiative and repair the dreadful potholes just a few feet from the gas works (please see photographs). The depressing reality is that I know the potholes won’t be repaired. Please please please can someone from the council intervene. The road is closed, the equipment is there, the workers with the skills have been hired. I can see the work they have done is of a high standard. Please can someone ask them to fix the potholes in the middle of the street. If this gas works is completed and the equipment is packed up and moved away without addressing the problem, I am going to make a monumental fuss. Please could someone in the council take control of this. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Highview Road, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Keverne Road, Elmstead, Greater London

item.title: It’s not Japanese knotweed but equally bad, 15th June
item.description:
Whilst this is not Japanese Knotweed, it’s just as serious - black bamboo has shot up in Pickhurst park bottom of the park left side along the hedge. I don’t know how long it’s been there but I went to insect blackberries on the left hand hedge and noticed it straight away. I did not see a direct option to report this so doing it against another invasive species, the knotweed I’ve experienced black bamboo directly myself. It grows very fast and spreads underground and can spread across a whole field in a year if left unchecked not to mention the damage it can cause Please dig it up before it spreads right across the park. Do not take this lightly this species can grow metres in days !! I’ve attached photos and can quite believe what you see above ground will be nothing compared to what is a foot or even two feet below ground, that’s how it spreads so quickly Photos attached Regards Darryl Ramsay

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Draven Close, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
